understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by getting every goal tile marked with a '+' occupied by a selectable piece at the same time (i.e., place pieces onto all '+' targets simultaneously).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is a currently-selected piece. CLICK on a reachable/valid tile causes the selected piece to relocate directly to that clicked coordinate if the destination is legal (not a wall/blocked); if illegal, nothing happens. MOVE_UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT attempts to move the selected piece in that direction; if the adjacent path is blocked by an obstacle/wall or otherwise invalid, the piece does not move. Some tiles (e.g., [35,17] in this run) appear to be blocked/unreachable (CLICK and movement attempts had no effect). Switching between positions like [12,18] and [21,21] via CLICK suggests those squares were valid destinations, while movement like MOVE_RIGHT from [21,21] was blocked. Each action consumes 1 step from a finite per-level step budget; reaching 0 ends the run without a win if goals are not all occupied.

orient_history: Movement seems grid-based in 3-pixel (1 cell) steps, but walls fully surround pieces; likely need to change selection via CLICK to move the other piece or interact to unlock movement.

Hypothesis: Pieces cannot slide via MOVE due to being enclosed; progress is made by CLICKing target positions to relocate the currently selected piece (likely snapping to valid empty cells). Need to place a selectable onto the goal region by clicking within it (cell coords -> pixel coords).

CLICK teleports/moves the selected piece to the clicked position only if that destination cell is reachable/valid (not inside wall/blocked). Movement via MOVE_* shifts by the piece size (3px=1 cell) but is currently impossible because the piece is fully surrounded by wall tiles; likely need to select/move the other piece or click directly onto a goal tile if unobstructed.

Hypothesis: The only effective interaction is CLICK-to-teleport the currently selected piece onto an existing selectable 'anchor' position. Winning likely requires moving/placing a selectable onto the goal; since movement is blocked, we must CLICK the goal area in case it acts as a valid destination/anchor for teleport/placement.

Hypothesis: CLICK on another selectable swaps/teleports the currently selected piece to that selectable’s position. Direct movement is blocked in this enclosed region, so to occupy the goal we likely need to select/move the other selectable next (or chain via selecting it) and attempt to reach the goal if any selectable is allowed to be placed onto goal by CLICK once selected/active.

New hypothesis: CLICK teleports/moves the currently selected piece directly to the clicked coordinate only if that destination is reachable/valid (not blocked). However, once moved into a fully enclosed wall pocket (here at [18,21]), normal MOVE actions are blocked in all directions, so I must switch to MOVE actions now per forced reframe to test if any movement is possible despite the blocked flags (or to trigger some mechanism).

Hypothesis: Movement is grid-stepped by 1 cell (3 pixels) per MOVE_*. CLICK teleports the selected piece to the clicked empty cell if reachable/valid; clicks into walls/invalid areas do nothing. Need to occupy the goal area with a selectable; since current piece is stuck, switch to moving the other selectable toward the goal using CLICK to reposition.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* attempts to slide the selected 3x3 piece by one cell (3 pixels) but collisions with walls stop movement. CLICK can reposition the selected piece only to valid/connected empty cells or onto another selectable’s position; direct clicking into the goal area is blocked by intervening walls/partition. Since P is trapped, we should switch selection back to the other piece (at [9,21]) which may have mobility to navigate toward the goal and potentially open a path or occupy it.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable swaps/teleports the currently selected piece to the clicked selectable's position. To win, we must occupy all goal tiles simultaneously; with two pieces and 9 goal tiles this likely means the engine treats the entire '+' region as a single goal area needing at least one piece on it, or only one of the '+' tiles is actually required. We should try moving/placing a piece onto the goal by selecting the other piece (S) and moving it if it has mobility.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches/teleports the active (selected) piece to that selectable’s anchor position. MOVE_* attempts to slide the selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) but is blocked by walls; current selected at [21,21] is stuck. To make progress, switch selection to the other selectable at [9,21] and then attempt movement/positioning toward the goal area (if any path exists).

World seems to have discrete 'anchor' positions for teleport/move via CLICK; normal MOVE actions are blocked by surrounding wall geometry at current anchor. To reach the goal, we likely must CLICK onto intermediate anchors that form a path to the goal area; goal may require a selectable to land exactly on its anchor/top-left pixel position.

New hypothesis: CLICK teleports the selected piece to the clicked location only if that destination is reachable/valid (not inside walls). Movement is grid-based in 3-pixel steps but the currently selected piece is trapped; progress requires switching selection to the other piece (at [9,21]) and moving/teleporting it toward the goal area.

Clicks teleport the selected piece to the clicked cell if that destination is a valid empty spot; movement by MOVE_* is often blocked by walls in the narrow corridor. To reach the goal, we likely must CLICK directly onto a goal tile within the '+' area. Prior click at [35,17] failed likely due to misalignment (must click exact cell origin / inside free space) or blocked because target overlaps wall; goal cells should be empty so try clicking the goal area's top-left cell origin aligned to 3px grid.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable swaps/teleports selection (and possibly the active piece) between existing selectable anchors; direct movement is only possible if not enclosed, but current anchor at [21,21] is fully walled. Need to reposition both selectables onto the goal area, likely by selecting the other anchor then attempting a CLICK onto goal to move there if line-of-sight/valid space.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects/teleports the currently-selected piece to the clicked selectable’s anchor position (swap/teleport between selectables). Direct MOVE is grid-step (3px) but currently blocked by walls, so progress requires changing which piece is selected (to S) and then attempting a different interaction from its location. Goal likely requires placing BOTH selectables onto goal tiles simultaneously, but only one goal region exists; it may accept multiple pieces within the 3x3 + area or require at least one piece to overlap it.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable swaps/teleports the currently selected piece to the clicked selectable’s position (as observed between [21,21] and [9,21]). Direct movement is impossible due to surrounding wall enclosure; progress likely requires changing which piece is selected (by clicking the other selectable) and then testing if any click can move/teleport into the goal area.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a reachable/valid location teleports the currently selected piece to that location if not blocked; arrow moves are blocked by surrounding walls. To win, need a selectable to occupy the goal region at [35,17] (pixel) / near the + tiles.

New hypothesis: Movement with MOVE_* is normal grid stepping but both selectables are enclosed in separate wall pockets, so moving will never work. Progress likely requires switching selection to the other selectable (via CLICK), then using MOVE_* (a different action type than repeated CLICK) to see if that piece has any available movement and can reach the goal pocket.

Movement with MOVE_* is standard grid-step (3 pixels) but currently impossible due to surrounding walls. CLICK seems to move the selected piece only to specific valid anchor positions (likely other selectable's position, or predefined snap points) and will fail if target is invalid/blocked. To win, we must get a selectable onto the goal; likely requires selecting the other selectable or using CLICK to swap/control it, since current P cannot move.

Movement with arrows shifts selected piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) but is blocked by walls. CLICK attempts to move the selected piece to the clicked pixel/cell; it succeeds only if the destination is a valid empty spot (not inside wall/outside bounds) and likely must fit the whole 3x3 piece. To win, we need both selectables occupying goal tiles simultaneously; since P is trapped, the only way is to CLICK-teleport each selectable onto goal cells.

Click teleports/moves the selected piece directly to a clicked reachable/open tile, but clicks on blocked/invalid targets do nothing. Movement commands move in 3-pixel increments (1 cell) when not blocked. To win, we likely need to position both selectables onto goal tiles simultaneously; currently only clicking between the two selectable positions has been effective, suggesting limited reachable open area (corridor) and that selecting/clicking the other selectable may switch which piece is controllable.

Hypothesis: Pieces can be repositioned via CLICK to certain reachable/valid empty locations (possibly along open corridors), while MOVE attempts are grid-based (3px) and often blocked by walls. To win, we must place BOTH selectables onto goal tiles simultaneously; goal is a 5x5 region so both 3x3 pieces likely need to overlap within it, or at least occupy goal cells. Since the current selected piece is immobile by MOVE, we should use CLICK to place it onto/near the goal, then move the other selectable similarly.

Hypothesis: CLICK on another selectable instantly switches/teleports the selected piece to that selectable’s anchor position; movement only works when not enclosed by walls. To reach the goal region (near x~35,y~17), we likely must use the other selectable at [12,21] (less boxed-in) and then move/position it onto the goal; currently selected piece at [21,21] cannot move at all.

New hypothesis: the piece is in a sealed compartment and cannot be moved by MOVE due to surrounding walls; progress requires switching which selectable is active (possibly via MOVE keys cycling focus or bumping into something), rather than moving the currently selected piece by CLICK. Since CLICK spamming didn’t help and MOVE is required by forced reframe, I will try a MOVE action to see if it changes selection or triggers a swap even when blocked.

Hypothesis: CLICK on another selectable teleports/moves the currently selected piece to that selectable’s position (effectively swapping/stacking selection anchor), but CLICK on empty/goal does nothing unless it’s a selectable. Movement is grid-stepped by 3 pixels, but both selectables appear trapped by walls, so reaching the goal likely requires using CLICK to reposition to the other selectable and then attempt a different move sequence from there.

Hypothesis: CLICK teleports the currently selected piece to the clicked location only if that destination area is not blocked by walls and likely within reachable empty space. Since P is boxed in, we should instead select/move the other selectable S and try to place it onto the goal region via CLICK directly onto the goal tile.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects/teleports the currently-controlled piece to the clicked selectable's position (piece-swap/warp between selectables). Direct MOVE only works when not enclosed by walls. Since P is trapped at [12,18], we should switch control/position to the other selectable at [21,21] and attempt to navigate it toward the goal area, potentially using CLICK-based warps to reposition.

Hypothesis: Movement by MOVE_* is grid-based in 3-pixel steps but blocked by walls; CLICK teleports/switches selection to an existing selectable at the clicked position (works when clicking [12,18] or [12,21]) but cannot move into walls/goal if path or occupancy rules prevent it. To win, a selectable must be positioned within the goal area; since P is trapped, we should switch control to the other selectable (S) and try moving it toward the goal zone.

World seems to allow CLICK-based repositioning only onto certain valid anchor tiles (likely selectable start anchors / special nodes). Arrow moves are grid-steps of 3px but currently impossible due to being enclosed by walls. Since we must occupy every goal tile with a selectable, but we cannot place onto the goal directly, the remaining viable approach is to switch which piece is selected and attempt a different CLICK behavior with the other selectable.

New hypothesis: the puzzle is unwinnable from this state because the only movable mechanism is swapping/teleporting between the two selectables via CLICK, but both pieces are confined behind walls and neither can be moved toward the goal using MOVE actions. Since a different action type is required now, I will attempt a MOVE anyway to confirm there is no hidden shift/push behavior when blocked.

CLICK teleports/moves the selected piece to the clicked coordinate only if that destination is a valid empty floor cell (not inside wall/blocked). Movement actions shift by the piece size (3px = 1 cell) but are blocked by adjacent walls; current selected piece is stuck. Winning likely requires placing a selectable onto the goal; with 1 step left we must attempt a valid CLICK directly onto a goal tile with the other selectable if selection changes, otherwise move selected there if possible.

With steps remaining at 0, the engine will not allow any effective action; movement/clicks cannot change state now, so the run is effectively ended without meeting the win condition (goal not occupied).

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by getting every goal tile marked with a '+' occupied by a selectable piece at the same time (i.e., place pieces onto all '+' targets simultaneously).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is a currently-selected piece. CLICK on a reachable/valid tile causes the selected piece to relocate directly to that clicked coordinate if the destination is legal (not a wall/blocked); if illegal, nothing happens. MOVE_UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT attempts to move the selected piece in that direction; if the adjacent path is blocked by an obstacle/wall or otherwise invalid, the piece does not move. Some tiles (e.g., [35,17] in this run) appear to be blocked/unreachable (CLICK and movement attempts had no effect). Switching between positions like [12,18] and [21,21] via CLICK suggests those squares were valid destinations, while movement like MOVE_RIGHT from [21,21] was blocked. Each action consumes 1 step from a finite per-level step budget; reaching 0 ends the run without a win if goals are not all occupied.
